creator: niedbalski@gmail.com
text:
# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#       latex-supertabular/
#       latex-supertabular/pkg-descr
#       latex-supertabular/Makefile
#       latex-supertabular/pkg-list
#       latex-supertabular/distinfo
#
echo c - latex-supertabular/
mkdir -p latex-supertabular/ > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - latex-supertabular/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >latex-supertabular/pkg-descr <<
'END-of-latex-supertabular/pkg-descr'
XThe supertabular bundle defines the supertabular and
Xsupertabular* environments. They make it possible to have a tabular
Xthat spans multiple pages. Each page is its own tabular environment,
Xthus the various parts may have different widths.
X
X
XWWW:http://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/supertabular/
X
END-of-latex-supertabular/pkg-descr
echo x - latex-supertabular/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >latex-supertabular/Makefile <<
'END-of-latex-supertabular/Makefile'
XPORTNAME=      latex-supertabular
XPORTVERSION=    1
XPORTREVISION=   1
XCATEGORIES=     print
XMASTER_SITES=
ftp://tug.ctan.org/pub/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/supertabular/
XDISTFILES=      supertabular.dtx supertabular.ins ${DOC_FILES}
XDIST_SUBDIR=    ${PORTNAME}
X
XMAINTAINER=     niedbalski@gmail.com
XCOMMENT=        A LaTeX package for have a tabular that spans multiple pages
X
XBUILD_DEPENDS=  mktexlsr:${PORTSDIR}/print/teTeX-base \
X                ${TEXMFLOCAL_LSR}:${PORTSDIR}/print/tex-texmflocal
XRUN_DEPENDS=    latex:${PORTSDIR}/print/teTeX-base \
X                ${TEXMFLOCAL_LSR}:${PORTSDIR}/print/tex-texmflocal
XPLIST_SUB=      TEXMFLOCAL=${TEXMFLOCAL} MKTEXLSR=${MKTEXLSR} \
X                CLASSDIR=${CLASSDIR}
XUSE_GMAKE=      YES
XTEXMFLOCAL=     share/texmf-local
XTEXMFLOCAL_LSR= ${LOCALBASE}/${TEXMFLOCAL}/ls-R
XMKTEXLSR=       ${LOCALBASE}/bin/mktexlsr
XTEX_BIN=        ${LOCALBASE}/bin/latex
XCLASSDIR=       ${TEXMFLOCAL}/tex/latex/${PORTNAME:S/^latex-//}
XWRKSRC=         ${WRKDIR}/${PORTNAME:S/^latex-//}
XCLASS_FILES=    supertabular.dtx supertabular.sty supertabular.ins
XDOC_FILES=      README MANIFEST CATALOG supertabular.pdf
X
Xdo-extract:
X       ${MKDIR} ${WRKSRC}
X       cd ${DISTDIR}/${DIST_SUBDIR} && ${CP} ${DISTFILES} ${WRKSRC}
X
Xdo-build:
X       cd ${WRKSRC} && ${TEX_BIN} supertabular.ins
X
Xdo-install:
X       ${MKDIR} ${PREFIX}/${CLASSDIR}
X       ${INSTALL_DATA} ${CLASS_FILES:S@^@${WRKSRC}/@} ${PREFIX}/${CLASSDIR}
X.if !defined(NOPORTDOCS)
X       ${MKDIR} ${DOCSDIR}
X       ${INSTALL_DATA} ${DOC_FILES:S@^@${WRKSRC}/@} ${DOCSDIR}
X.endif
X
Xpost-install:
X       @${MKTEXLSR}
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-latex-supertabular/Makefile
echo x - latex-supertabular/pkg-list
sed 's/^X//' >latex-supertabular/pkg-list <<
'END-of-latex-supertabular/pkg-list'

echo x - latex-supertabular/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >latex-supertabular/distinfo <<
